text: The surgeon demonstrates the use of the ender wrist to pick up rings from pegs at multiple vertical levels. The left arm slides the ring off the peg using the instrument tips, hands it to the other arm, and returns to the horizontal peg. The surgeon twists fingers to align the ring with the peg, emphasizing coordinated arm movement and stability by keeping forearms on the armrest. The camera view remains stable as the arms move together, and the surgeon pushes up to get an overview of the flashing ring on the third level.

The video demonstrates common mistakes during the task, including excessive force when pulling the ring off the peg, indicated by the left arm turning red. It shows poor economy of motion with unnecessary movements before reaching the target, and overreaching when the peg is close instead of navigating the camera. The handoff error is highlighted, where the left arm fails to release the ring promptly during transfer to the other arm.
